title: judge reliability — self-consistency + order-shuffle
note: Pre-registration. These thresholds and sample frames are fixed BEFORE the LLM eval runs; the recorded verdicts (e6_selfconsistency.jsonl / e6_ordershuffle.jsonl) and the derived e6_summary.json must be judged against exactly these floors. E6 measures agreement (reliability), never accuracy against a gold label.

gate_rule.rationale: kappa>=0.6 is Landis-Koch 'substantial'; the raw-agreement co-floor is prevalence-robust because kappa deflates under the cohort's skewed base rates (advisory-heavy).

exp_a_self_consistency.gate_signal: self_consistency.pass -> whether R5's new Pass-2 sub-answer (asserted_capability_confirmed) is worth adding
exp_a_self_consistency.agreement_unit: pass3 decision in {block, advisory, dropped, indeterminate}
exp_a_self_consistency.sample_frame: runs/adjudication_corpus.jsonl rows with criterion in {G6, E4, T3}
exp_a_self_consistency.plan_snapshot: each sampled finding's ticket plan text is snapshotted once from rebar's replay-derived description into runs/e6_selfconsistency_inputs.jsonl; a finding whose plan is unretrievable is skipped and topped up from the cohort remainder to hold N=50

exp_b_order_shuffle.gate_signal: order_shuffle.pass -> prerequisite for trusting R3's new (order-exposed) container criterion
exp_b_order_shuffle.permutation_rule: permutation 0 = identity; permutations 1..2 = distinct section orders from random.Random(seed).shuffle
exp_b_order_shuffle.excluded_from_frame: 5 of 19 corpus plans have < 3 top-level sections (one =2, four =1)
exp_b_order_shuffle.container_subset: descriptive only, NOT gated (only 5 of 19 inputs are containers -> under-powered; order-sensitivity is a general judge property, so a stable general-plan result is the prerequisite)

infra_indeterminate_exclusion.exp_a: a vote whose pass3 decision == 'indeterminate' (three_pass.pass3_decide returns this ONLY when Pass-2 produced no verdict) is an execution failure -> dropped and re-run
infra_indeterminate_exclusion.exp_b: a verdict == 'INDETERMINATE' whose coverage carries llm_unavailable or verify_failed is an execution failure -> dropped and re-run; a genuine judge-INDETERMINATE (neither flag) is KEPT as its own agreement category

retry_cap.policy: collect up to vote_target substantive votes within attempt_budget calls; a subject that cannot reach the target is recorded as an explicit excluded row (e6_*_excluded.jsonl) and NEVER silently padded

total_expected_llm_spend.note: deliberately bounded sub-samples, not the full 400-finding adjudication corpus nor the 628-row outcome corpus; excludes re-runs for infra-INDETERMINATE draws (bounded by the retry cap)
